layer1_client.jwt_auth: Client sends JWT token in socket.io connection auth
socket_events: typing - Notify room user is typing
stop-typing - Notify room user stopped typing

layer2_authentication.process: 1. Extract token from socket.handshake.auth.token 2. Verify with jwt.verify() 3. Attach user info to socket 4. Reject if invalid

layer4_socket_handlers.room_concept: Each conversation gets unique room: [userId1, userId2].sort().join('_')
layer4_socket_handlers.message_flow: 1. User sends message 2. Server validates room membership 3. Server calls chatService.saveMessage() 4. Server broadcasts only to room members via io.to(roomId).emit()

layer6_rest_api.validation: Each endpoint validates user ownership/access before operation

socket_message_flow.step3: Validation: Check user, validate room membership
socket_message_flow.step4: Service Layer: chatService.saveMessage() saves to DB
socket_message_flow.step6: Clients in room: Receive message in real-time

rest_api_flow.step2: Server: authMiddleware extracts userId from JWT
rest_api_flow.step3: Route Handler: Validates user access to chat
rest_api_flow.step4: Service Layer: chatService.getMessages() retrieves from DB

socket_optimization.room_based: io.to(roomId) instead of io.emit() - avoids broadcasting to all users
socket_optimization.memory: Active users tracked efficiently in object, not array

database_optimization.indexes: Fast queries on frequently accessed fields
database_optimization.aggregation: MongoDB $group and $count for analytics - no loading into memory
database_optimization.lean: chat.find().lean() returns plain objects (faster than full documents)
database_optimization.limits: Max 100 messages per request - prevents large result sets

api_optimization.pagination: limit and skip parameters for message retrieval
api_optimization.validation: Limit capped at 100 to prevent DOS
api_optimization.caching: MongoDB handles query optimization with indexes

scalability_considerations.current_setup: Single Node.js server with in-memory socket manager
scalability_considerations.next_step: For multiple servers, migrate socketManager.activeUsers to Redis
scalability_considerations.benefits: Socket instances across servers share rooms and events

v1_issues.issue1: Global broadcasts - all users receive all messages
v1_issues.issue4: No service layer - logic in route handlers

v2_solutions.solution1: Room-based communication - only room members receive

before_deploy: Update socket connection URL in client
scaling_preparation: When server load increases, migrate to Redis
Implement room message queue if needed
Consider message compression for large payloads

testing_summary.integration_tests: Test socket events with mock JWT tokens
testing_summary.api_tests: Test REST endpoints with Postman collection
testing_summary.security_tests: Verify ownership checks, room isolation, auth validation
testing_summary.load_tests: Test with multiple concurrent users when scaling
